title: Signed Numbers, BCD, and Gray Code

content.value: Hardware needs a way to represent sign using only 0s and 1s. The three classic methods are sign-magnitude, 1's complement, and 2's complement. 2's complement is what virtually all modern processors use because it makes addition and subtraction use the same circuitry.

items: Sign-magnitude: leftmost bit is the sign (0=+, 1=-), remaining bits are magnitude
1's complement: flip every bit of the positive number to get the negative
2's complement: flip every bit, then add 1

content.value:
Represent -9 in 8-bit 2's complement:
+9 = 00001001
flip bits = 11110110
add 1     = 11110111  <- this is -9
To compute A - B, take the 2's complement of B and add it to A. Discard any carry out of the most significant bit.

BCD encodes each decimal digit separately using 4 bits (0000-1001). It wastes some bit patterns (1010-1111 are invalid) but makes converting to/from human-readable decimal trivial, which is why it's used in digital clocks and calculators.
Add the two 4-bit groups using normal binary addition. If the 4-bit result is greater than 9, or a carry was generated out of that group, add 6 (0110) to correct it back into valid BCD.

BCD: 8 + 7
1000 + 0111 = 1111 (15, invalid BCD digit)
Add 0110 correction: 1111 + 0110 = 1 0101
Result: carry 1, digit 0101 -> reads as '15' in BCD (1 0101)

Gray code is a binary sequence where only one bit changes between consecutive values. This prevents glitches in circuits like rotary encoders, where multiple bits flipping at slightly different times could be misread.

Binary -> Gray conversion: g(n) = b(n)
g(i) = b(i) XOR b(i+1)  for each lower bit

Example: Binary 0111 -> Gray 0100
Exam favorite: convert a 4-bit binary count sequence (0000 to 1111) entirely into Gray code. Practice this table until it's automatic.

quiz.question: Which representation lets adders handle both addition and subtraction with the same hardware?
In BCD addition, when do you add a correction of 0110?
options: Only when the 4-bit sum exceeds 9 or produces a carry

quiz.question: What is the defining property of Gray code?
options: Only one bit changes between consecutive values
